_comment: Project Note — Sample seed data
How to use:
1) The default owner (suzy / Suzy123$) is auto-seeded by the app on first login;
you do NOT need to import this file to get a working app.
2) This file is only useful if you want some realistic projects/notes/users
to play with. The recommended approach is a small Node script using the
Firebase Admin SDK:
// seed.js
const admin = require('firebase-admin');
const data  = require('./sample-data.json');
admin.initializeApp({ credential: admin.credential.applicationDefault() });
const db = admin.firestore();
async function run() {
for (const u of data.users)     await db.doc(`users/${u.uid}`).set(u);
for (const u of data.usernames) await db.doc(`usernames/${u.username}`).set(u);
for (const p of data.projects)  await db.doc(`projects/${p.id}`).set(p);
for (const g of data.groups)    await db.doc(`groups/${g.id}`).set(g);
for (const n of data.notes)     await db.doc(`notes/${n.id}`).set(n);
for (const s of data.shares)    await db.doc(`shares/${s.id}`).set(s);
console.log('Seeded.');
run().catch(console.error);
3) Replace the placeholder UIDs (uid_*) with REAL uids generated by Firebase Auth.
The app expects /users/{uid} doc.id to match the Firebase Auth uid for the user.
You can either: a) create accounts via the registration form, then update this
JSON with the resulting uids; or b) create them with admin.auth().createUser(...)
in your seed script and use the returned uid.
4) Date strings in this file should be turned into Firestore Timestamps in the
seed script: admin.firestore.Timestamp.fromDate(new Date(value)).
